steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank ends mid-clause: Jetties can sometimes have the opposite [blank] — an incomplete independent clause. The right side is: obstructing the natural flow of sand along the shore can lead to increased erosion in some areas — a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for 'though'
steps.content:
When connecting independent clauses with though, the pattern is: [IC], though [IC] — a comma before though with the clause continuing. Option B correctly applies this pattern, placing a comma before though to join the two clauses. The other options all violate the rule: A adds a semicolon after though, C places a semicolon before though instead of a comma, and D adds an unnecessary comma after though.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
